reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all the components of the informal statement: `m ≤ Ring.jacobson R` encodes "m is an ideal in the Jacobson radical", `G₁.Monic` matches "G₁ is monic", `G₁.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk m)` is precisely the reduction of G₁ modulo m (giving an element of (R/m)[x]), and both "generate the unit ideal" conditions are correctly expressed as `Ideal.span {...} = ⊤`. The one point of potential concern is the use of `CommRing R` vs. just "ring" — but this is a classical commutative algebra theorem (a Nakayama-style argument relying on R[x]/(G₁) being a finitely generated R-module), and the informal language (using 𝔪, "unit ideal," and the Jacobson-radical framing) unambiguously refers to the commutative setting. The commutativity is an implicit premise standard in this context, not a strengthening of scope. All quantifier structure, types, and logical relationships match.
